questions.question: Consider a finite extensive game with perfect information, where two players make decisions sequentially. What is a key characteristic of the game tree in such games?
questions.hint: The game tree has a unique path to the Nash equilibrium.

questions.question: Suppose a player in an extensive game with perfect information makes a decision based solely on the history of the game, including their opponent's moves. What is the strategic form of the game?
questions.hint: It is a finite, zero-sum, zero-player game.

questions.question: In an extensive game with perfect information, what is the concept of a 'subgame perfect equilibrium'?
questions.hint: It is a Nash equilibrium that holds for every subgame of the original game.
